get.description: Return the currently authenticated user profile from the access token.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a user. Returns `{ deleted: true }` on success,
raises 404 if the user does not exist.

Permanently delete a store and cascade to its scoped collections.

Cascades to `store_inventory`, `sales`, `attendance_records`, and
`daily_assignments` so the `storeId` no longer appears anywhere. Returns
`{ deleted: true }` on success; raises 404 if the store does not exist.

post.description:
Allocate N units of a food item to a store.

Resolves the food item's active recipe, computes the required ingredients
per unit × N, and atomically deducts from the store's inventory.
Rolls back the entire allocation if any ingredient is insufficient.
Now writes a persistent record to the ``allocations`` collection.

get.description: Return today's attendance record for the calling user (if any).

post.description: Worker clock-in for the current day. Idempotent — second call updates existing record.
Worker clock-out for the current day.

get.description: Admin/manager view of one employee's attendance over a date range.
All employees' attendance for the range, grouped by userId.

post.description: Admin override — set status for a specific date.

get.description:
Per-ingredient allocated quantities for a store.

Aggregates ``deductions[].required`` across the matching allocations so the
worker UI can show the *allocated* amount per ingredient (e.g. "10 buns
allocated today") rather than the cumulative shelf stock from
``store_inventory``.

post.description:
Reclaim the unsold leftover ingredients from an active allocation.

Refunds per-ingredient shares of ``remaining = allocated - sold`` back to
the master pool and marks the allocation as ``RECLAIMED``. Idempotent.

get.description: Legacy per-food moving-average forecast. Use ``/projected-daily`` instead.
Per-day forecast for the next ``days`` days (consumed by the dashboard 7-day chart).
Download a CSV of the chosen report. PDF is not implemented yet.

Static manifest describing the read-only bridge: tools, allowed
collections, blocked operators, and limits. Mirrors the introspection
surface of the MongoDB MCP server so any MCP-compatible client can be
pointed at /api/v1/mcp/* safely.

schema.title: Response Health Check Api V1 Health Get

StoreCreateRequest.title: StoreCreateRequest
StoreCreateRequest.description:
Payload accepted on `POST /api/v1/stores`.

The frontend (Vite) sends flat fields: `name, code, address, city, phone,
openingTime, closingTime`. We accept those directly and keep `type` optional
so the backend can default it to ``RETAIL`` in the service layer. The legacy
nested ``location`` / ``openingHours`` shapes are still accepted so existing
seed data and any older clients keep working.

StoreStatusUpdateRequest.title: StoreStatusUpdateRequest
StoreStatusUpdateRequest.description:
Payload accepted on `PATCH /api/v1/stores/{id}/status`.

Accepts either ``status`` (Literal) or ``isActive`` (bool). At least one must
be provided; they are equivalent (``isActive=True`` ↔ ``status="OPEN"``).

StoreUpdateRequest.title: StoreUpdateRequest
StoreUpdateRequest.description: Payload accepted on `PUT /api/v1/stores/{id}`. All fields are optional.
